mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hike periods with statistical significance (p < 0.05), or rolling Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp, or event study alpha is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand strength, driving corporate earnings revisions and positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce statistically significant positive 60-day forward returns (t-stat < 1.96), or the interaction term loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity constraints and supply chain bottlenecks, which compress profit margins and generate negative monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in over 60% of cases, or the mechanism shows no regime-dependent predictive power in a Markov-switching model.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 4.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that suppresses equity risk premiums, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes in a quantile regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth alongside a stable unemployment rate indicates robust labor demand without wage-price spiral risks, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and stable UNRATE yields negative or flat 60-day forward returns in out-of-sample periods, or the interaction coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant positive correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns (Rank IC < 0.02), or returns are negative during high growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid industrial production expansion during a post-shock recovery phase signals pent-up demand fulfillment and margin expansion, driving positive weekly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO surges during recovery phases fail to predict positive 5-day S&P 500 returns, with event study alpha remaining negative or statistically insignificant across multiple recovery episodes.
